stages.name: unit_tests
stages.evidence: test/run_command_test 'run rejects --backend with a dedicated error and the backend docs state the honest re-tag, list-mutants-only scope' asserts run_command.parseArgs(--backend zir|air) returns the dedicated error.BackendNotInRun (before the fix it was the generic error.UnknownOption -- captured failing as 'expected error.BackendNotInRun, found error.UnknownOption'), that a real run option still parses, and that docs/ZIR_BACKEND.md and docs/AIR_BACKEND.md contain the honest 're-tag' behavior and the 'list-mutants' scope. Full suite green (503 tests).

stages.name: real_binary_rejection
stages.evidence: The built binary rejects `run --backend zir` with exit 2 and the clear message 'error[ZNTL_CLI_INVALID_OPTION]: --backend is list-mutants-only; run always uses the stable AST backend (the experimental ZIR/AIR backends re-tag AST candidates and do no IR analysis)' -- an explicit, documented rejection, not a silent no-op.

stages.name: mutation_checks
stages.reason: task 114 aligns experimental-backend docs with reality and makes run --backend an explicit usage error; the behavioral fix is proven by a parser test that fails under the pre-fix generic UnknownOption. Full self-mutation dogfood is gated to task 085.

residual_risk: The docs now state ZIR/AIR are relabel prototypes that re-tag AST candidates with no IR analysis and that --backend is list-mutants-only; the doc assertions in the test check the honest keywords ('re-tag', 'list-mutants') are present rather than pinning exact prose, so a future reword that keeps those terms stays green. Real ZIR/AIR IR lowering remains a documented future effort (non-goal here).

run rejects the CLI --backend flag explicitly. A config `[backend] default = zir` (experimental opt-in) is still ignored by run (run always uses the stable AST backend); that config-level path is outside this task's finding (which is about the --backend CLI surface) and src/config.zig is not in this task's allowed files, so it is left as-is and documented as 'run always uses the stable AST backend'.
The list-mutants --backend zir|air relabel behavior is unchanged (it re-tags AST candidates); only its documentation honesty and the run-side rejection changed.
